expectations.text: Decided the marketing skill applies and used it
expectations.evidence: Opens with a stated assumption and produces structured cold-email copy with subject/preview/body, consistent with skill execution.

expectations.text: Subject line is present and under ~50 characters
expectations.evidence: Subject: 'Churn you can see 30 days early' — 31 characters.

expectations.text: Includes preview/preheader text complementary to the subject
expectations.evidence: Preview: 'Most cancellations are predictable — if you're looking.' complements the subject.

expectations.text: Opening is relevance-first (no generic throat-clearing like 'I hope this finds you well')
expectations.evidence: Opens 'Most SaaS churn isn't a surprise.' — straight to the reader's problem.

expectations.text: Body is one idea and under ~120 words
expectations.evidence: Single idea (early churn signals); body ~95 words.

expectations.text: Has exactly one low-friction CTA (yes/no or permission ask), not a 30-minute meeting demand
expectations.evidence: CTA is a yes/no permission ask: 'Worth me sending a 2-line example — yes or no?' No meeting demand.

expectations.text: States assumptions inline rather than blocking with questions (scoped/low-stakes task)
expectations.evidence: Leads with 'Assuming: targeting B2B SaaS founders... goal = a reply, not a booked call.'
